 adhunā cāśritā maghāḥ|
491.ldw_fc: sapta-ṛṣīṇām ; tu ; yau ; pūrvau ; dṛśyete ; uditau ; divi ; tayoḥ ; tu ; madhye ; nakṣatram ; dṛśyate ; yat ; samam ; niśi ; tena ; eva ; ṛṣayaḥ ; yuktāḥ ; tiṣṭhanti ; abda-śatam ; nṛṇām ; te ; tvadīye ; dvijāḥ ; kāle ; adhunā ; ca ; āśritāḥ ; maghāḥ
491.words_en_fc: of the constellation of the seven sages (the constellation known to Westerners as Ursa Major); and; which two stars; first; are seen; risen; in the sky; of the two (named Pulaha and Kratu); and; between; the lunar mansion; is seen; which; on the same line of celestial longitude, as their midpoint; in the night sky; with that lunar mansion; indeed; the seven sages; are connected; they remain; one hundred years; of human beings; these seven sages; in your; the elevated brāhmaṇas; in the time; now; and; are situated; in the asterism Maghā.
491.yw_zh: 在北斗七星形成的七贤星座中，普拉哈和夸图首先升起在夜空中。假如由北向南的一条线通过它们的中间点，那么这根线所通过的星宿可以说就是那个时候该星座的执政星群。七大圣贤将与那个星宿连接一百个地球年。当前，在你有生之年，他们处在称为玛嘎的naksatra中。
491.yw_en: Of the seven stars forming the constellation of the seven sages, Pulaha and Kratu are the first to rise in the night sky. If a line running north and south were drawn through their midpoint, whichever of the lunar mansions this line passes through is said to be the ruling asterism of the constellation for that time. The Seven Sages will remain connected with that particular lunar mansion for one hundred human years. Currently, during your lifetime, they are situated in the nakṣatra called Maghā.

491.ldw:
viṣṇor bhagavato bhānuḥ
 kṛṣṇākhyo ’sau divaṁ gataḥ
tadāviśat kalir lokaṁ
 pāpe yad ramate janaḥ|
491.ldw_fc: viṣṇoḥ ; bhagavataḥ ; bhānuḥ ; kṛṣṇa-ākhyaḥ ; asau ; divam ; gataḥ ; tadā ; aviśat ; kaliḥ ; lokam ; pāpe ; yat ; ramate ; janaḥ
491.words_en_fc: of Viṣṇu; the Supreme Personality of Godhead; the sun; known as Kṛṣṇa; He; to the spiritual sky; having returned; then; entered; the Age of Kali; this world; in sin; in which age; take pleasure; the people.
491.yw_zh: 至尊主维施努像太阳一样光辉灿烂，以奎师那著称。他返回灵性天穹后，卡历便进入这世界，那时，人们开始对罪恶活动乐此不疲。
491.yw_en: The Supreme Lord, Viṣṇu, is brilliant like the sun and is known as Kṛṣṇa. When He returned to the spiritual sky, Kali entered this world, and people then began to take pleasure in sinful activities.

491.ldw:
yāvat sa pāda-padmābhyāṁ
 spṛśan āste ramā-patiḥ
tāvat kalir vai pṛthivīṁ
 parākrantuṁ na cāśakat|
491.ldw_fc: yāvat ; saḥ ; pāda-padmābhyām ; spṛśan ; āste ; ramā-patiḥ ; tāvat ; kaliḥ ; vai ; pṛthivīm ; parākrantum ; na ; ca ; aśakat
491.words_en_fc: as long as; He, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa; with His lotus feet; touching; remained; the husband of the goddess of fortune; for that long; the Age of Kali; indeed; the earth; to overcome; not; and; was able.
491.yw_zh: 只要圣主奎师那，幸运女神的丈夫，依然以他的莲花足触碰地球，卡历就无力征服这星球。
491.yw_en: As long as Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the husband of the goddess of fortune, touched the earth with His lotus feet, Kali was powerless to subdue this planet.
491.yz_zh: 尽管甚至在主奎师那莅临地球期间，卡历就已经通过杜尤丹及其同党的不虔诚活动微微踏进地球，但圣主奎师那一直抑制住了卡历的影响。直至主奎师那离开地球之后，卡历才日益猖撅。
491.yz_en: Although even during the time of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s presence on earth Kali had entered the earth to a slight extent through the impious activities of Duryodhana and his allies, Lord Kṛṣṇa consistently suppressed Kali’s influence. Kali could not flourish until Lord Kṛṣṇa had left the earth.

491.ldw:
yadā devarṣayaḥ sapta
 maghāsu vicaranti hi
tadā pravṛttas tu kalir
 dvādaśābda-śatātmakaḥ|
491.ldw_fc: yadā ; deva-ṛṣayaḥ sapta ; maghāsu ; vicaranti ; hi ; tadā ; pravṛttaḥ ; tu ; kaliḥ ; dvādaśa ; abda-śata ; ātmakaḥ
491.words_en_fc: when; the seven sages among the demigods; in the lunar mansion Maghā; are traveling; indeed; then; begins; and; the Age of Kali; twelve; centuries [These twelve hundred years of the demigods equal 432,000 earth years]; consisting of.
491.yw_zh: 当七贤的星座经过玛嘎宿，卡历年代便告开始。卡历年代的时长等于半神人的1200年（432000地球年）。
491.yw_en: When the constellation of the seven sages is passing through the lunar mansion Maghā, the Age of Kali begins. It comprises twelve hundred years of the demigods.

491.ldw:
yadā maghābhyo yāsyanti
 pūrvāṣāḍhāṁ maharṣayaḥ
tadā nandāt prabhṛty eṣa
 kalir vṛddhiṁ gamiṣyati|
491.ldw_fc: yadā ; maghābhyaḥ ; yāsyanti ; pūrva-āṣāḍhām ; mahā-ṛṣayaḥ ; tadā ; nandāt ; prabhṛti ; eṣaḥ ; kaliḥ ; vṛddhim ; gamiṣyati
491.words_en_fc: when; from Maghā; they will go; to the next lunar mansion, Pūrvāṣāḍhā; the seven great sages; then; beginning from Nanda; and his descendants; this; Age of Kali; maturity; will attain.
491.yw_zh: 当伟大圣贤中的萨普塔尔锡星座从玛嘎宿运行到普尔瓦萨达宿，卡历将显示出他的全部力量，这要从南达王及其王朝开始。
491.yw_en: When the great sages of the Saptarṣi constellation pass from Maghā to Pūrvāsāḍhā, Kali will have his full strength, beginning from King Nanda and his dynasty.

491.ldw:
yasmin kṛṣṇo divaṁ yātas
 tasminn eva tadāhani
pratipannaṁ kali-yugam
 iti prāhuḥ purā-vidaḥ|
491.ldw_fc: yasmin ; kṛṣṇaḥ ; divam ; yātaḥ ; tasmin ; eva ; tadā ; ahani ; pratipannam ; kali-yugam ; iti ; prāhuḥ ; purā ; vidaḥ
491.words_en_fc: on which; Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa; to the spiritual world; gone; on that; same; then; day; obtained; the Age of Kali; thus; they say; of the past; the experts.
491.yw_zh: 那些科学地了解过去的人宣布，就在圣主奎师那隐迹回到灵性世界的那一日，卡历年代的影响便开始了。
491.yw_en: Those who scientifically understand the past declare that on the very day that Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa departed for the spiritual world, the influence of the Age of Kali began.
491.yz_zh: 尽管实际上卡历-尤嘎本来是在主奎师那莅临地球期间开始，但这个堕落的年代必须恭顺地等待至尊人格首神离去。
491.yz_en: Although technically Kali-yuga was to begin during the time of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s presence on earth, this fallen age had to wait meekly for the departure of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

491.ldw:
divyābdānāṁ sahasrānte
 caturthe tu punaḥ kṛtam
bhaviṣyati tadā nṝṇāṁ
 mana ātma-prakāśakam|
491.ldw_fc: divya ; abdānām ; sahasra ; ante ; caturthe ; tu ; punaḥ ; kṛtam ; bhaviṣyati ; tadā ; nṝṇām ; manaḥ ; ātma-prakāśakam
491.words_en_fc: of the demigods; years; of one thousand; at the end; in the fourth age, Kali; and; again; the Satya-yuga; will be; then; of men; the minds; self-luminous.
491.yw_zh: 卡历-尤嘎持续一千个天堂年之后，萨提亚-尤嘎将再次展示。那时，全体人类的心意将变得自明。
491.yw_en: After the one thousand celestial years of Kali-yuga, the Satya-yuga will manifest again. At that time the minds of all men will become self-effulgent.

491.ldw:
ity eṣa mānavo vaṁśo
 yathā saṅkhyāyate bhuvi
tathā viṭ-śūdra-viprāṇāṁ
 tās tā jñeyā yuge yuge|
491.ldw_fc: iti ; eṣaḥ ; mānavaḥ ; vaṁśaḥ ; yathā ; saṅkhyāyate ; bhuvi ; tathā ; viṭ ; śūdra ; viprāṇām ; tāḥ tāḥ ; jñeyāḥ ; yuge yuge
491.words_en_fc: thus (in the cantos of this Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam); this; descending from Vaivasvata Manu; the dynasty; as; it is enumerated; upon the earth; in the same way; of the vaiśyas; śūdras; and brāhmaṇas; the situations of each; are to be understood; in each age.
491.yw_zh: 如此，我已讲述了闻名于地球的玛努王朝。一个人能以同样的方式去研究生活在各个年代的外夏、舒铎和布茹阿玛纳的历史。
491.yw_en: Thus I have described the royal dynasty of Manu, as it is known on this earth. One can similarly study the history of the vaiśyas, śūdras and brāhmaṇas living in the various ages.
491.yz_zh: 就像在众多王朝中，有的君王显赫崇高。有的名不见经传，有的圣贤，有的邪恶，同样，各种各样的人类品质体现在知识分子、商贾和劳动阶层中。
491.yz_en: Just as the dynasty of kings includes exalted and insignificant, virtuous and wicked monarchs, varieties of human character are found in the intellectual, commercial and laboring orders of society.

491.ldw:
eteṣāṁ nāma-liṅgānāṁ
 puruṣāṇāṁ mahātmanām
kathā-mātrāvaśiṣṭānāṁ
 kīrtir eva sthitā bhuvi|
491.ldw_fc: eteṣām ; nāma ; liṅgānām ; puruṣāṇām ; mahā-ātmanām ; kathā ; mātra ; avaśiṣṭānām ; kīrtiḥ ; eva ; sthitā ; bhuvi
491.words_en_fc: of these; their names; which are the only means of remembering them; of the personalities; who were great souls; the stories; merely; whose remaining portion; the glories; only; are present; on the earth.
491.yw_zh: 这些是伟大灵魂的人物，现在只是流芳百世。他们仅仅名垂青史，美名传扬地球。
491.yw_en: These personalities, who were great souls, are now known only by their names. They exist only in accounts from the past, and only their fame remains on the earth.
491.yz_zh: 尽管某人可能自诩为了不起的强大领袖，但是当他最终撒手人表，长长的名册上仅留下他的名字而已。换言之，依附物质世界中的权力与地位毫无用处。
491.yz_en: Although one may consider oneself to be a great, powerful leader, he will ultimately end up as a name in a long list of names. In other words, it is useless to be attached to power and position in the material world.

491.ldw:
devāpiḥ śāntanor bhrātā
 maruś cekṣvāku-vaṁśa-jaḥ
kalāpa-grāma āsāte
 mahā-yoga-balānvitau|
491.ldw_fc: devāpiḥ ; śāntanoḥ ; bhrātā ; maruḥ ; ca ; ikṣvāku-vaṁśa-jaḥ ; kalāpa-grāme ; āsāte ; mahā ; yoga-bala ; anvitau
491.words_en_fc: Devāpi; of Mahārāja Śāntanu; the brother; Maru; and; born in the dynasty of Ikṣvāku; in the village Kalāpa; the two of them are living; great; with mystic power; endowed.
491.yw_zh: 玛哈茹阿吉•商塔努的兄弟戴瓦批以及玛茹，伊克施瓦库的后裔，二者拥有巨大的神秘力量，甚至时至今日都一直生活在卡拉帕村庄。
491.yw_en: Devāpi, the brother of Mahārāja Śāntanu, and Maru, the descendant of Ikṣvāku, both possess great mystic strength and are living even now in the village of Kalāpa.

491.ldw:
tāv ihaitya kaler ante
 vāsudevānuśikṣitau
varṇāśrama-yutaṁ dharmaṁ
 pūrva-vat prathayiṣyataḥ|
491.ldw_fc: tau ; iha ; etya ; kaleḥ ; ante ; vāsudeva ; anuśikṣitau ; varṇa-āśrama ; yutam ; dharmam ; pūrva-vat ; prathayiṣyataḥ
491.words_en_fc: they (Maru and Devāpi); to human society; returning; of the Age of Kali; at the end; by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vāsudeva; instructed; the divine system of occupational and spiritual orders of society; comprising; the code of eternal religion; just as previously; they will promulgate.
491.yw_zh: 在卡历年代末，这两位已经直接从至尊人格首神华苏戴瓦那里获得教诲的君王，将回到人类社会，重建跟往昔一样具有varna和asrama（社会职责和灵性晋阶的神圣制度）划分之特点的人类永恒的宗教。
491.yw_en: At the end of the Age of Kali, these two kings, having received instruction directly from the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vāsudeva, will return to human society and reestablish the eternal religion of man, characterized by the divisions of varṇa and āśrama, just as it was before.
491.yz_zh: 根据这节诗和上节诗，两位将在卡历-尤嘎结束后重建人类文化的伟大君王已降临地球，他们在耐心等待着为主维施努做奉献服务。
491.yz_en: According to this and the previous verse, the two great kings who will reestablish human culture after the end of Kali-yuga have already descended to the earth, where they are patiently waiting to render their devotional service to Lord Viṣṇu.

491.ldw:
kṛtaṁ tretā dvāparaṁ ca
 kaliś ceti catur-yugam
anena krama-yogena
 bhuvi prāṇiṣu vartate|
491.ldw_fc: kṛtam ; tretā ; dvāparam ; ca ; kaliḥ ; ca ; iti ; catuḥ-yugam ; anena ; krama ; yogena ; bhuvi ; prāṇiṣu ; vartate
491.words_en_fc: Satya-yuga; Tretā-yuga; Dvāpara-yuga; and; Kali-yuga; and; thus; the cycle of four ages; by this; sequential; pattern; in this world; among living beings; goes on continuously.
491.yw_zh: 四个年代的循环——萨提亚、特瑞塔、杜瓦帕尔和卡历——在众生中，在这地球上，一直不断地循环往复。
491.yw_en: The cycle of four ages — Satya, Tretā, Dvāpara and Kali — continues perpetually among living beings on this earth, repeating the same general sequence of events.

491.ldw:
rājann ete mayā proktā
 nara-devās tathāpare
bhūmau mamatvaṁ kṛtvānte
 hitvemāṁ nidhanaṁ gatāḥ|
491.ldw_fc: rājan ; ete ; mayā ; proktāḥ ; nara-devāḥ ; tathā ; apare ; bhūmau ; mamatvam ; kṛtvā ; ante ; hitvā ; imām ; nidhanam ; gatāḥ
491.words_en_fc: O King Parīkṣit; these; by me; described; kings; and; other human beings; upon the earth; possessiveness; exerting; in the end; giving up; this world; destruction; met.
491.yw_zh: 我亲爱的帕瑞克西特王，我说的所有这些君王和其他人来到地球，施加影响，取得所有权，但最终他们都必须放弃这世界，遭逢毁灭。
491.yw_en: My dear King Parīkṣit, all these kings I have described, as well as all other human beings, come to this earth and stake their claims, but ultimately they all must give up this world and meet their destruction.

491.ldw:
kṛmi-viḍ-bhasma-saṁjñānte
 rāja-nāmno ’pi yasya ca
bhūta-dhruk tat-kṛte svārthaṁ
 kiṁ veda nirayo yataḥ|
491.ldw_fc: kṛmi ; viṭ ; bhasma ; saṁjñā ; ante ; rāja-nāmnaḥ ; api ; yasya ; ca ; bhūta ; dhruk ; tat-kṛte ; sva-artham ; kim ; veda ; nirayaḥ ; yataḥ
491.words_en_fc: of worms; stool; and ashes; the designation; in the end; going by the name “king”; even though; of which (body); and; of living beings; an enemy; for the sake of that body; his own best interest; what; does he know; punishment in hell; because of which.
491.yw_zh: 即使一个人的身体现在冠以“王”的名号，但最终那名号将是“蠕虫”、“粪便”或“灰烬”。为了自身的躯体而伤害其他生物的人，由于他的活动正在完全将他引向地狱，他怎能明了他自身的自我福祉呢？
491.yw_en: Even though a person’s body may now have the designation “king,” in the end its name will be “worms,” “stool” or “ashes.” What can a person who injures other living beings for the sake of his body know about his own self-interest, since his activities are simply leading him to hell?
491.yz_zh: 死后，身体要么被掩埋或被虫子吃掉，要么被扔到街上或森林，让动物吃了，然后排成粪便，或者要么被焚成灰烬，因此，人不该用自身的短暂之躯伤害其他生物的身体，为自己铺设通往地狱的路。在这节诗，bhuta一词还包括非人类生命形体，这些生命形体也是神的创造物。人应该摈弃一切心怀嫉妒的暴行，学会以奎师那知觉的方式看到神在一切中。
491.yz_en: After death, the body may be buried and eaten by worms, or it may be thrown in the street or forest to be eaten by animals who will pass out its remnants as stool, or it may be burned and converted into ashes. Therefore one should not pave his way to hell by using his temporary body to injure the bodies of other living beings. In this verse the word bhūta includes nonhuman life forms, who are also creatures of God. One should give up all envious violence and learn to see God in everything by the process of Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

491.ldw:
kathaṁ seyam akhaṇḍā bhūḥ
 pūrvair me puruṣair dhṛtā
mat-putrasya ca pautrasya
 mat-pūrvā vaṁśa-jasya vā|
491.ldw_fc: katham ; sā iyam ; akhaṇḍā ; bhūḥ ; pūrvaiḥ ; me ; puruṣaiḥ ; dhṛtā ; mat-putrasya ; ca ; pautrasya ; mat-pūrvā ; vaṁśa-jasya ; vā
491.words_en_fc: how; this same; unbounded; earth; by the predecessors; my; by the personalities; held in control; of my son; and; of the grandson; now under my sway; of the descendant; or.
491.yw_zh: （物欲横流的君王想：）“这无边的疆土世世代代被我的祖辈掌控，如今由我主宰。我如何能让这江山社稷一直由我的子孙后代相传不息呢？”
491.yw_en: [The materialistic king thinks:] “This unbounded earth was held by my predecessors and is now under my sovereignty. How can I arrange for it to remain in the hands of my sons, grandsons and other descendants?”
491.yz_zh: 这个例子充分揭露愚蠢的拥有之念。
491.yz_en: This is an example of foolish possessiveness.

491.ldw:
tejo-’b-anna-mayaṁ kāyaṁ
 gṛhītvātmatayābudhāḥ
mahīṁ mamatayā cobhau
 hitvānte ’darśanaṁ gatāḥ|
491.ldw_fc: tejaḥ ; ap ; anna ; mayam ; kāyam ; gṛhītvā ; ātmatayā ; abudhāḥ ; mahīm ; mamatayā ; ca ; ubhau ; hitvā ; ante ; adarśanam ; gatāḥ
491.words_en_fc: fire; water; and earth; composed of; this body; accepting; with the sense of “I”; the unintelligent; this earth; with the sense of “my”; and; both; giving up; ultimately; disappearance; they have obtained.
491.yw_zh: 尽管愚人把这具由土、水、火组成的躯体接受为“我”，把地球当做“我的”，但总而言之，他们最终抛下他们的躯体和地球，撒手人寰，逐渐被世人忘却。
491.yw_en: Although the foolish accept the body made of earth, water and fire as “me” and this earth as “mine,” in every case they have ultimately abandoned both their body and the earth and passed away into oblivion.
491.yz_zh: 虽然灵魂永恒，伹我们所谓的家庭传统和世俗名望必将湮没无闻，渐为忘却。
491.yz_en: Although the soul is eternal, our so-called family tradition and earthly fame will certainly pass into oblivion.

491.ldw:
ye ye bhū-patayo rājan
 bhuñjate bhuvam ojasā
kālena te kṛtāḥ sarve
 kathā-mātrāḥ kathāsu ca|
491.ldw_fc: ye ye ; bhū-patayaḥ ; rājan ; bhuñjate ; bhuvam ; ojasā ; kālena ; te ; kṛtāḥ ; sarve ; kathā-mātrāḥ ; kathāsu ; ca
491.words_en_fc: whatever; kings; O King Parīkṣit; enjoy; the world; with their power; by the force of time; they; have been made; all; mere accounts; in various histories; and.
491.yw_zh: 我亲爱的帕瑞克西特王，所有这些凭一已之力设法享受地球的君王，在时间力量的驱使下，消逝成一串串历史记录，仅此而已。
491.yw_en: My dear King Parīkṣit, all these kings who tried to enjoy the earth by their strength were reduced by the force of time to nothing more than historical accounts.
491.yz_zh: 本诗节的rajan“王啊”一词意义深远。帕瑞克西特•玛哈茹阿吉正在为离开躯体，重返家园，回归首神做准备，舒卡戴瓦•勾斯瓦米，他最为仁慈的灵性导师，通过说明王位最终微不足道，以摧毁他可能对王位哪怕略微的依附。籍着灵性导师的无缘仁慈，人才为重返家园，回归首神做好充分准备。灵性导师教人放下对物质幻象的执着，把maya的王国抛之脑后。虽然舒卡戴瓦•勾斯瓦米在本章一语道破物质世界的所谓荣耀，却显示着灵性导师的无缘仁慈，要把他臣服的门徒带回首神的国度外琨塔。
491.yz_en:
The word rājan, “O King,” is significant in this verse. Parīkṣit Mahārāja was preparing to give up his body and go back home, back to Godhead, and Śukadeva Gosvāmī, his most merciful spiritual master, devastated any possible attachment that he might have to the position of king by showing the ultimate insignificance of such a position. By the causeless mercy of the spiritual master one is prepared to go back home, back to Godhead. The spiritual master teaches one to relax one’s strong grip on material illusion and leave the kingdom of māyā behind. Although Śukadeva Gosvāmī speaks very bluntly within this chapter about the so-called glory of the material world, he is exhibiting the causeless mercy of the spiritual master, who takes his surrendered disciple back to the kingdom of Godhead, Vaikuṇṭha.

Thus end the purports of the humble servants of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupāda to the Twelfth Canto, Second Chapter, of the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, entitled “The Symptoms of Kali-yuga.”

492.ldw:
śrī-śuka uvāca|dṛṣṭvātmani jaye vyagrān
 nṛpān hasati bhūr iyam
aho mā vijigīṣanti
 mṛtyoḥ krīḍanakā nṛpāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: śrī-śukaḥ uvāca ; dṛṣṭvā ; ātmani ; jaye ; vyagrān ; nṛpān ; hasati ; bhūḥ ; iyam ; aho ; mā ; vijigīṣanti ; mṛtyoḥ ; krīḍanakāḥ ; nṛpāḥ
492.words_en_fc: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said; observing; of herself; in conquest; busily engaged; the kings; she laughs; the earth; this; ah; me; they are desiring to conquer; of death; playthings; the kings.
492.yw_zh: 舒卡戴瓦·哥斯瓦米说：看到地球上的君王们忙于试图征服她，地球本人不禁仰天长笑。她说道：“看啊，这些君王是多么想征服我，但他们实际上只是死亡手中的玩偶罢了。
492.yw_en: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Seeing the kings of this earth busy trying to conquer her, the earth herself laughed. She said: “Just see how these kings, who are actually playthings in the hands of death, are desiring to conquer me.

492.ldw:
kāma eṣa narendrāṇāṁ
 moghaḥ syād viduṣām api
yena phenopame piṇḍe
 ye ’ti-viśrambhitā nṛpāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: kāmaḥ ; eṣaḥ ; nara-indrāṇām ; moghaḥ ; syāt ; viduṣām ; api ; yena ; phena-upame ; piṇḍe ; ye ; ati-viśrambhitāḥ ; nṛpāḥ
492.words_en_fc: lust; this; of the rulers of men; the failure; becomes; who are wise; even; by which (lust); comparable to ephemeral bubbles; in this lump; who; perfectly trusting; the kings.
492.yw_zh: ”人类伟大的统治者们，甚至那些博学之士也因物质色欲遭遇挫折与失败。在色欲的驱使下，这些君王寄予这称为躯体的一堆赘肉以巨大的希望与信心，尽管物质躯体俨如水上的泡沫，转瞬即逝。
492.yw_en: “Great rulers of men, even those who are learned, meet frustration and failure because of material lust. Driven by lust, these kings place great hope and faith in the dead lump of flesh called the body, even though the material frame is as fleeting as bubbles of foam on water.

492.ldw:
pūrvaṁ nirjitya ṣaḍ-vargaṁ
 jeṣyāmo rāja-mantriṇaḥ
tataḥ saciva-paurāpta-
 karīndrān asya kaṇṭakān|evaṁ krameṇa jeṣyāmaḥ
 pṛthvīṁ sāgara-mekhalām
ity āśā-baddha-hṛdayā
 na paśyanty antike ’ntakam|
492.ldw_fc: pūrvam ; nirjitya ; ṣaṭ-vargam ; jeṣyāmaḥ ; rāja-mantriṇaḥ ; tataḥ ; saciva ; paura ; āpta ; kari-indrān ; asya ; kaṇṭakān ; evam ; krameṇa ; jeṣyāmaḥ ; pṛthvīm ; sāgara ; mekhalām ; iti ; āśā ; baddha ; hṛdayāḥ ; na paśyanti ; antike ; antakam
492.words_en_fc: first of all; conquering; the five senses and the mind; we will conquer; the royal ministers; then; the personal secretaries; the citizens of the capital; the friends; the elephant keepers; ridding ourselves of; the thorns; in this way; gradually; we shall conquer; the earth; the ocean; whose girdle; thus thinking; by hopes; bound up; their hearts; they do not see; nearby; their own end.
492.yw_zh: “君王和政治家们心想：首先，我要征服我的感官与心意；接着，我要征服我的大臣们，剔除我的顾问、臣民、朋友、亲戚和驯象师们的眼中钉。如此，我将逐渐征服整个地球。由于这些首脑野心勃勃，故而未看到死亡近在咫尺，悄然等待。
492.yw_en: “Kings and politicians imagine: ‘First I will conquer my senses and mind; then I will subdue my chief ministers and rid myself of the thorn-pricks of my advisors, citizens, friends and relatives, as well as the keepers of my elephants. In this way I will gradually conquer the entire earth.’ Because the hearts of these leaders are bound by great expectations, they fail to see death waiting nearby.
492.yz_zh: 为了满足—己的权力欲，坚决的政治家、独裁者和军事首脑们严格自律，做严苛的苦行和巨大的栖牲。然后，他们带领国民坚决奋斗，要掌控海陆空。尽管政治家及其追随者们很快就要命赴黄泉一因为在这世上生死不可避免一他们却依然固我，疯狂地追逐昙花一现的荣耀。
492.yz_en: To satisfy their greed for power, determined politicians, dictators and military leaders undergo severe austerities and sacrifice, with much self-discipline. Then they lead their great nations in a struggle to control the sea, land, air and space. Although the politicians and their followers will soon be dead — since birth and death are all inevitable in this world — they persist in their frenetic struggle for ephemeral glory.

492.ldw:
samudrāvaraṇāṁ jitvā
 māṁ viśanty abdhim ojasā
kiyad ātma-jayasyaitan
 muktir ātma-jaye phalam|
492.ldw_fc: samudra-āvaraṇām ; jitvā ; mām ; viśanti ; abdhim ; ojasā ; kiyat ; ātma-jayasya ; etat ; muktiḥ ; ātma-jaye ; phalam
492.words_en_fc: bounded by the ocean; having conquered; me; they enter; the ocean; by their strength; how much; of victory over the self; this; liberation; of victory over the self; the fruit.
492.yw_zh: ”在征服所在疆土之后，这些傲慢的君王强行闯入海洋，试图将其征服。他们为了政治目的而严格自律，如此又有何用？自律的真正目的是取得灵性解脱。“
492.yw_en: “After conquering all the land on my surface, these proud kings forcibly enter the ocean to conquer the sea itself. What is the use of their self-control, which is aimed at political exploitation? The actual goal of self-control is spiritual liberation.”

492.ldw:
yāṁ visṛjyaiva manavas
 tat-sutāś ca kurūdvaha
gatā yathāgataṁ yuddhe
 tāṁ māṁ jeṣyanty abuddhayaḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yām ; visṛjya ; eva ; manavaḥ ; tat-sutāḥ ; ca ; kuru-udvaha ; gatāḥ ; yathā-āgatam ; yuddhe ; tām ; mām ; jeṣyanti ; abuddhayaḥ
492.words_en_fc: whom; giving up; indeed; human beings; their sons; also; O best of the Kurus; gone away; just as they had originally come; in battle; that; me, the earth; they try to conquer; unintelligent.
492.yw_zh: 库茹族的佼佼者啊，地球继续道：”虽然过往的伟人及其后裔离我而去，告别尘世，就像当初他们无助地来到这世间一般，但如今，愚人却在试图征服我。
492.yw_en: O best of the Kurus, the earth continued as follows: “Although in the past great men and their descendants have left me, departing from this world in the same helpless way they came into it, even today foolish men are trying to conquer me.

492.ldw:
mat-kṛte pitṛ-putrāṇāṁ
 bhrātṝṇāṁ cāpi vigrahaḥ
jāyate hy asatāṁ rājye
 mamatā-baddha-cetasām|
492.ldw_fc: mat-kṛte ; pitṛ-putrāṇām ; bhrātṝṇām ; ca ; api ; vigrahaḥ ; jāyate ; hi ; asatām ; rājye ; mamatā ; baddha ; cetasām
492.words_en_fc: for the sake of me; between fathers and sons; among brothers; and; also; conflict; arises; indeed; among the materialistic; for political rule; by the sense of possession; bound up; whose hearts.
492.yw_zh: 为了征服我，物质主义者互相争斗。父子对立，手足相残，因为他们的心被拥有政权的欲望占据着。
492.yw_en: “For the sake of conquering me, materialistic persons fight one another. Fathers oppose their sons, and brothers fight one another, because their hearts are bound to possessing political power.

492.ldw:
mamaiveyaṁ mahī kṛtsnā
 na te mūḍheti vādinaḥ
spardhamānā mitho ghnanti
 mriyante mat-kṛte nṛpāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: mama ; eva ; iyam ; mahī ; kṛtsnā ; na ; te ; mūḍha ; iti vādinaḥ ; spardhamānāḥ ; mithaḥ ; ghnanti ; mriyante ; mat-kṛte ; nṛpāḥ
492.words_en_fc: mine; indeed; this; land; entire; not; yours; you fool; thus speaking; quarreling; each other; they kill; they are killed; for my sake; kings.
492.yw_zh: 政治首脑们彼此挑战说：这整片土地是我的！不是你的，你这傻瓜！就这样，他们互相攻击致死。
492.yw_en: “Political leaders challenge one another: ‘All this land is mine! It’s not yours, you fool!’ Thus they attack one another and die.
492.yz_zh:
这节诗说得很明了，世俗的政客心态挑起世上无数冲突。例如，就在我们准备《圣典博伽瓦谭》的译稿时，英国和阿根廷两国军队正在激烈作战，争夺小小的福克兰群岛。

事实上至尊主才是一切土地的拥有者。诚然，即便在有神知觉的世界也存在着政治边界。但是，在那种有神知觉的氛围中，政治紧张局势大幅度缓解，各地区的人们和睦相处，互相尊重。
492.yz_en:
This verse describes with brilliant clarity the mundane political mentality that provokes innumerable conflicts in the world. For example, as we prepare this translation of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, British and Argentine military forces are bitterly fighting over the tiny Falkland Islands.

The fact is that the Supreme Lord is the proprietor of all land. Of course, even in a God-conscious world political boundaries exist. But in such a God-conscious atmosphere political tensions are greatly eased, and people of all lands welcome each other and respect each other’s right to live in peace.

492.ldw:
pṛthuḥ purūravā gādhir
 nahuṣo bharato ’rjunaḥ
māndhātā sagaro rāmaḥ
 khaṭvāṅgo dhundhuhā raghuḥ|tṛṇabindur yayātiś ca
 śaryātiḥ śantanur gayaḥ
bhagīrathaḥ kuvalayāśvaḥ
 kakutstho naiṣadho nṛgaḥ|hiraṇyakaśipur vṛtro
 rāvaṇo loka-rāvaṇaḥ
namuciḥ śambaro bhaumo
 hiraṇyākṣo ’tha tārakaḥ|anye ca bahavo daityā
 rājāno ye maheśvarāḥ
sarve sarva-vidaḥ śūrāḥ
 sarve sarva-jito ’jitāḥ|mamatāṁ mayy avartanta
 kṛtvoccair martya-dharmiṇaḥ
kathāvaśeṣāḥ kālena
 hy akṛtārthāḥ kṛtā vibho|
492.ldw_fc: pṛthuḥ purūravāḥ gādhiḥ ; nahuṣaḥ bharataḥ arjunaḥ ; māndhātā sagaraḥ rāmaḥ ; khaṭvāṅgaḥ dhundhuhā raghuḥ ; tṛṇabinduḥ yayātiḥ ca ; śaryātiḥ śantanuḥ gayaḥ ; bhagīrathaḥ kuvalayāśvaḥ ; kakutsthaḥ naiṣadhaḥ nṛgaḥ ; hiraṇyakaśipuḥ vṛtraḥ ; rāvaṇaḥ ; loka-rāvaṇaḥ ; namuciḥ śambaraḥ bhaumaḥ ; hiraṇyākṣaḥ ; atha ; tārakaḥ ; anye ; ca ; bahavaḥ ; daityāḥ ; rājānaḥ ; ye ; mahā-īśvarāḥ ; sarve ; sarva-vidaḥ ; śūrāḥ ; sarve ; sarva-jitaḥ ; ajitāḥ ; mamatām ; mayi ; avartanta ; kṛtvā ; uccaiḥ ; martya-dharmiṇaḥ ; kathā-avaśeṣāḥ ; kālena ; hi ; akṛta-arthāḥ ; kṛtāḥ ; vibho
492.words_en_fc: Mahārājas Pṛthu, Purūravā and Gādhi; Nahuṣa, Bharata and Kārtavīrya Arjuna; Māndhātā, Sagara and Rāma; Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhuhā and Raghu; Tṛṇabindu and Yayāti; Śaryāti, Śantanu and Gaya; Bhagīratha and Kuvalayāśva; Kakutstha, Naiṣadha and Nṛga; Hiraṇyakaśipu and Vṛtrāsura; Rāvaṇa; who made the whole world cry; Namuci, Śambara and Bhauma; Hiraṇyākṣa; and; Tāraka; others; as well; many; demons; kings; who; great controllers; all of them; all-knowing; heroes; all; all-conquering; unconquerable; possessiveness; for me; they lived; expressing; to a great degree; subject to the laws of birth and death; remaining merely as historical narrations; by the force of time; indeed; incomplete in perfecting their desires; they have been made; O Lord.
492.yw_zh: 玛哈茹阿吉普瑞图，普茹阿阿瓦和嘎迪，纳胡沙，巴茹阿塔，卡尔特维尔亚·阿尔诸那，曼达塔，萨嘎茹阿，茹阿玛，卡特旺嘎，敦度哈，茹阿古，垂纳宾度，雅亚提，沙尔亚提，商塔努，嘎亚，巴给茹阿塔，库瓦拉亚刷 ，卡库蒋塔，奈沙达，尼瑞噶，黑冉亚卡西普和维陀苏如阿，使整个世界生灵涂炭的人茹阿瓦那，纳穆齐，商巴尔，鲍玛，黑冉亚克沙和塔茹阿卡，以及许多其他拥有掌控他人之大能的恶魔与君王，全都无所不晓，勇敢英武，所向无敌，不可征服。然而，全能的主啊，尽管他们活着时想千方百计把我占为已有，但这些君王随着时间的流逝而隐没，只是成为一串串历史记录。他们中没有谁能永久地确立他们的统治权。”
492.yw_en: “Such kings as Pṛthu, Purūravā, Gādhi, Nahuṣa, Bharata, Kārtavīrya Arjuna, Māndhātā, Sagara, Rāma, Khaṭvāṅga, Dhundhuhā, Raghu, Tṛṇabindu, Yayāti, Śaryāti, Śantanu, Gaya, Bhagīratha, Kuvalayāśva, Kakutstha, Naiṣadha, Nṛga, Hiraṇyakaśipu, Vṛtra, Rāvaṇa, who made the whole world lament, Namuci, Śambara, Bhauma, Hiraṇyākṣa and Tāraka, as well as many other demons and kings who possessed great powers of control over others, were all full of knowledge, heroic, all-conquering and unconquerable. Nevertheless, O almighty Lord, although they lived their lives intensely trying to possess me, these kings were subject to the passage of time, which reduced them all to mere historical accounts. None of them could permanently establish their rule.”
492.yz_zh:
正如施瑞拉•施瑞达尔•斯瓦米说，也正如施瑞拉•维施瓦纳塔•査夸瓦尔提•塔库尔确证的，这里提到的茹阿玛王并非首神的化身茹阿玛禅铎。普瑞图•玛哈茹阿吉被认为是至尊人格首神的一位化身，他充分展示出地球君王的品质，声称拥有整个地球。然而，像普瑞图•玛哈茹阿吉这样的圣王代表至尊人格首神统治地球，然而，像黑冉亚卡西普或茹阿瓦那之类的恶魔却企图剥削利用地球，以求自身的感官享乐。尽管如此，无论圣王还是恶魔都得告别地球。如此，他们的政治霸权最终被时间力量抵消。

当代政治领袖们甚至做不到控制整个地球，他们的宫裕和智慧并非取之不尽，用之不竭。当代领袖们拥有一星半点的权力，本受短促的寿命，对生命存在缺乏深刻智慧，所以，他们无疑是挫败与方向错误的野心的象征。
492.yz_en:
According to Śrīla Śrīdhara Svāmī, and as confirmed by Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura, the King Rāma mentioned here is not the incarnation of Godhead Rāmacandra. Pṛthu Mahārāja is understood to be an incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who completely exhibited the characteristics of an earthly king, claiming proprietorship over the entire earth. A saintly king like Pṛthu Mahārāja, however, controls the earth on behalf of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, whereas a demon such as Hiraṇyakaśipu or Rāvaṇa tries to exploit the earth for his personal sense gratification. Nevertheless, both saintly kings and demons must leave the earth. In this way their political supremacy is ultimately neutralized by the force of time.

Modern political leaders cannot even temporarily control the entire earth, nor are their opulences and intelligence unlimited. Possessing hopelessly fragmented power, enjoying a minuscule life span, and lacking deep existential intelligence, modern leaders inevitably are symbols of frustration and misdirected ambition.

492.ldw:
kathā imās te kathitā mahīyasāṁ
 vitāya lokeṣu yaśaḥ pareyuṣām
vijñāna-vairāgya-vivakṣayā vibho
 vaco-vibhūtīr na tu pāramārthyam|
492.ldw_fc: kathāḥ ; imāḥ ; te ; kathitāḥ ; mahīyasām ; vitāya ; lokeṣu ; yaśaḥ ; pareyuṣām ; vijñāna ; vairāgya ; vivakṣayā ; vibho ; vacaḥ ; vibhūtīḥ ; na ; tu ; pārama-arthyam
492.words_en_fc: the narrations; these; unto you; have been spoken; of great kings; spreading; throughout all the worlds; their fame; who have departed; transcendental knowledge; and renunciation; with the desire for teaching; O mighty Parīkṣit; of words; the decoration; not; but; of the most essential purport.
492.yw_zh: 舒卡戴瓦·哥斯瓦米说：孔武有力的帕瑞克西特啊，我已向你讲述过这些伟大君王，他们曾名扬全世界，继而隐没告别尘世。我的真意是传授超然知识和弃绝精神。君王们的故事使得这些叙述生动有力，丰富多彩，但这些叙述本身并不构成终极知识。
492.yw_en: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O mighty Parīkṣit, I have related to you the narrations of all these great kings, who spread their fame throughout the world and then departed. My real purpose was to teach transcendental knowledge and renunciation. Stories of kings lend power and opulence to these narrations but do not in themselves constitute the ultimate aspect of knowledge.
492.yz_zh: 因为《圣典博伽瓦谭》中的一切叙述可把读者带到超然知识的完美之境，所以它们提供的全是至髙无上的灵性教导，尽管看起来讨论的是许多君王或其它世俗題旨。若跟奎师那紧密相连，一切普通题旨都会变成超然的讲述，具有能把读者带人生命的完美境界的威力。
492.yz_en: Since all the narrations of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam bring the reader to the perfection of transcendental knowledge, they all give supreme spiritual lessons though apparently dealing with kings or other mundane subject matter. In relation with Kṛṣṇa, all ordinary topics become transcendental narrations, with the power to bring the reader to the perfection of life.

492.ldw:
yas tūttamaḥ-śloka-guṇānuvādaḥ
 saṅgīyate ’bhīkṣṇam amaṅgala-ghnaḥ
tam eva nityaṁ śṛṇuyād abhīkṣṇaṁ
 kṛṣṇe ’malāṁ bhaktim abhīpsamānaḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yaḥ ; tu ; uttamaḥ-śloka ; guṇa ; anuvādaḥ ; saṅgīyate ; abhīkṣṇam ; amaṅgala-ghnaḥ ; tam ; eva ; nityam ; śṛṇuyāt ; abhīkṣṇam ; kṛṣṇe ; amalām ; bhaktim ; abhīpsamānaḥ
492.words_en_fc: which; on the other hand; of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is praised in transcendental verses; of the qualities; the recounting; is sung; always; which destroys everything inauspicious; that; indeed; regularly; one should hear; constantly; unto Lord Kṛṣṇa; untainted; devotional service; he who desires.
492.yw_zh: 渴望为主奎师那做纯粹奉献服务的人，应该聆听对主乌塔玛施娄卡的光辉品质的叙述，恒常歌颂他的光辉品质可摧毁一切不吉祥。奉献者应该在日常聚会中聆听这样的讲述，并且还应该全天候不断地聆听。
492.yw_en: The person who desires pure devotional service to Lord Kṛṣṇa should hear the narrations of Lord Uttamaḥśloka’s glorious qualities, the constant chanting of which destroys everything inauspicious. The devotee should engage in such listening in regular daily assemblies and should also continue his hearing throughout the day.
492.yz_zh: 关于主奎师那的任何话題都是吉祥而超然的，对主奎师那亲身活动的直接叙述无疑是至髙无上的倾听主题。这里的nityam—词指的是经常讨论关于主奎师那的话題，abhiksnam说的是恒常铭记这些规范的灵性经历。
492.yz_en: Since any topic related to Lord Kṛṣṇa is auspicious and transcendental, the direct narration of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s own activities, political and nonpolitical, is certainly the supreme subject matter for hearing. The word nityam here indicates regulated cultivation of the topics of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and abhīkṣṇam indicates constant remembrance of such regulated spiritual experiences.

492.ldw:
śrī-rājovāca|kenopāyena bhagavan
 kaler doṣān kalau janāḥ
vidhamiṣyanty upacitāṁs
 tan me brūhi yathā mune|
492.ldw_fc: śrī-rājā uvāca ; kena ; upāyena ; bhagavan ; kaleḥ ; doṣān ; kalau ; janāḥ ; vidhamiṣyanti ; upacitān ; tat ; me ; brūhi ; yathā ; mune
492.words_en_fc: King Parīkṣit said; by what; means; my dear lord; of the Age of Kali; the faults; living in Kali-yuga; people; will eradicate; accumulated; that; to me; please explain; fittingly; O sage.
492.yw_zh: 帕瑞克西特王说：我的主，生活在卡历年代的人如何能根除这个年代经年累积的污染？伟大的圣贤啊，请向我明示。
492.yw_en: King Parīkṣit said: My lord, how can persons living in the Age of Kali rid themselves of the cumulative contamination of this age? O great sage, please explain this to me.
492.yz_zh: 帕瑞克西特王是慈悲为怀的明君圣王。因此，在聆听过卡历年代可憎的品质之后，他自然询问诞生在这个年代的人如何能使自身免除卡历年代固有的污染。
492.yz_en: King Parīkṣit was a compassionate, saintly ruler. Thus, after hearing of the abominable qualities of the Age of Kali, he naturally inquired as to how those born in this age can free themselves of its inherent contamination.

492.ldw:
yugāni yuga-dharmāṁś ca
 mānaṁ pralaya-kalpayoḥ
kālasyeśvara-rūpasya
 gatiṁ viṣṇor mahātmanaḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yugāni ; yuga-dharmān ; ca ; mānam ; pralaya ; kalpayoḥ ; kālasya ; īśvara-rūpasya ; gatim ; viṣṇoḥ ; mahā-ātmanaḥ
492.words_en_fc: the ages of the universal history; the special qualities of each age; and; the measurement; of annihilation; and of universal maintenance; of time; the representation of the Personality of Godhead; the movement; of Lord Viṣṇu; the Supreme Soul.
492.yw_zh: 请讲解不同年代的宇宙史，每个年代的特性，宇宙的维系与毁灭期，以及时间的运转，时间是至尊灵魂，人格首神主维施努的直接代表。
492.yw_en: Please explain the different ages of universal history, the special qualities of each age, the duration of cosmic maintenance and destruction, and the movement of time, which is the direct representation of the Supreme Soul, the Personality of Godhead, Lord Viṣṇu.

492.ldw:
śrī-śuka uvāca|kṛte pravartate dharmaś
 catuṣ-pāt taj-janair dhṛtaḥ
satyaṁ dayā tapo dānam
 iti pādā vibhor nṛpa|
492.ldw_fc: śrī-śukaḥ uvāca ; kṛte ; pravartate ; dharmaḥ ; catuḥ-pāt ; tat ; janaiḥ ; dhṛtaḥ ; satyam ; dayā ; tapaḥ ; dānam ; iti ; pādāḥ ; vibhoḥ ; nṛpa
492.words_en_fc: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said; in the Satya-yuga, the age of truth; exists; religion; with four legs; of that age; by the people; maintained; truth; mercy; austerity; charity; thus; the legs; of mighty religion; O King.
492.yw_zh: 舒卡戴瓦·哥斯瓦米说：我亲爱的王，最初，在萨提亚-尤嘎，真诚年代，宗教的四条腿完整无缺地存在着，并为那个年代的人们所精心维护。宗教强有力的四条腿是真诚、仁慈、苦行和布施。
492.yw_en: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: My dear King, in the beginning, during Satya-yuga, the age of truth, religion is present with all four of its legs intact and is carefully maintained by the people of that age. These four legs of powerful religion are truthfulness, mercy, austerity and charity.
492.yz_zh:
就像有四个季节，地球有四个年代，每个年代持续好几十万年。第一个年代是萨提亚-尤嘎，在那个年代，乐善好施等好品质十分显著。

真正的布施，即这里说的danam，是指把无惧和自由给与他人，而并非以物质途径给他人一时的快乐或缓解。任何物质的"布施”将不可避免地被时间的巨轮砸碎。因此，唯有觉悟到自身永恒的存在超然于时间之上，人才会无惧无畏，而只有免除了物质欲望，才是真正的自由自在，因为这种自由自在能使人超脱自然法则的束缚。所以，真正的布施是帮助人们恢复他们永恒的灵性知觉。

这里用vibhu“强有力的”一词描述宗教，因为普遍的宗教原则跟至尊主本人没有不同，最终引人臻达他的国度。这里提到的品质——真诚、仁慈、苦行和布施一是虔诚生活的普遍且无宗派的方面。

在《圣典博伽瓦谭》第一篇，宗教的第四条腿是洁净。据施瑞拉•维施瓦纳塔•恰夸瓦尔提•塔库尔说，这是对本节诗中的danam—词的另一种定义。
492.yz_en:
Just as there are four seasons, there are four ages of the earth, each lasting hundreds of thousands of years. The first of these is Satya-yuga, when such good qualities as charity are prominent.

Actual charity, here referred to as dānam, is to award fearlessness and freedom to others, not to give them some material means of temporary pleasure or relief. Any material “charitable” arrangement will inevitably be crushed by the onward march of time. Thus only realization of one’s eternal existence beyond the reach of time can make one fearless, and only freedom from material desire constitutes real freedom, for it enables one to escape the bondage of the laws of nature. Therefore real charity is to help people revive their eternal, spiritual consciousness.

Religion is here referred to as vibhu, “the mighty,” because universal religious principles are not different from the Supreme Lord Himself and ultimately lead one to His kingdom. The qualities mentioned here — truthfulness, mercy, austerity and charity — are universal, nonsectarian aspects of pious life.

In the First Canto of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, the fourth leg of religion is listed as cleanliness. According to Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura, this is an alternative definition of the word dānam in the present context.

492.ldw:
santuṣṭāḥ karuṇā maitrāḥ
 śāntā dāntās titikṣavaḥ
ātmārāmāḥ sama-dṛśaḥ
 prāyaśaḥ śramaṇā janāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: santuṣṭāḥ ; karuṇāḥ ; maitrāḥ ; śāntāḥ ; dāntāḥ ; titikṣavaḥ ; ātma-ārāmāḥ ; sama-dṛśaḥ ; prāyaśaḥ ; śramaṇāḥ ; janāḥ
492.words_en_fc: self-satisfied; merciful; friendly; pacified; self-controlled; tolerant; enthused from within; possessed of equal vision; for the most part; endeavoring diligently (for self-realization); the people.
492.yw_zh: 萨提亚-尤嘎的人们大多自我满足，仁慈为怀，善待众生，怡然平和，头脑清醒，宽容忍耐。他们从内寻求快乐，平等看待一切，总是勤勉努力，追求灵性完美。
492.yw_en: The people of Satya-yuga are for the most part self-satisfied, merciful, friendly to all, peaceful, sober and tolerant. They take their pleasure from within, see all things equally and always endeavor diligently for spiritual perfection.
492.yz_zh: Sama-darsana，平等的眼光，其基础是觉知到斑驳陆离的物质的背后和居于众生心中的至尊灵魂。
492.yz_en: Sama-darśana, equal vision, is based on the perception of the Supreme Spirit behind all material variety and within all living entities.

492.ldw:
tretāyāṁ dharma-pādānāṁ
 turyāṁśo hīyate śanaiḥ
adharma-pādair anṛta-
 hiṁṣāsantoṣa-vigrahaiḥ|
492.ldw_fc: tretāyām ; dharma-pādānām ; turya ; aṁśaḥ ; hīyate ; śanaiḥ ; adharma-pādaiḥ ; anṛta ; hiṁsā ; asantoṣa ; vigrahaiḥ
492.words_en_fc: in the second age; of the legs of religion; one fourth; part; is lost; gradually; by the legs of irreligion; by falsity; violence; dissatisfaction; and quarrel.
492.yw_zh: 在特瑞塔-尤嘎，由于反宗教四大支柱，欺骗、暴力、不满和纷争的影响，宗教的腿以四分之一的比例递减。
492.yw_en: In Tretā-yuga each leg of religion is gradually reduced by one quarter by the influence of the four pillars of irreligion — lying, violence, dissatisfaction and quarrel.
492.yz_zh: 虚伪导致失去真诚，暴力使人丧失仁慈，不满使人轻视苦行，纷争削弱布施与洁净。
492.yz_en: By falsity truth is diminished, by violence mercy is diminished, by dissatisfaction austerity is diminished, and by quarrel charity and cleanliness are diminished.

492.ldw:
tadā kriyā-tapo-niṣṭhā
 nāti-hiṁsrā na lampaṭāḥ
trai-vargikās trayī-vṛddhā
 varṇā brahmottarā nṛpa|
492.ldw_fc: tadā ; kriyā ; tapaḥ ; niṣṭhāḥ ; na ati-hiṁsrāḥ ; na lampaṭāḥ ; trai-vargikāḥ ; trayī ; vṛddhāḥ ; varṇāḥ ; brahma-uttarāḥ ; nṛpa
492.words_en_fc: then (in the Tretā age); to ritualistic ceremonies; and to penances; devoted; not excessively violent; not wantonly desiring sense gratification; interested in the three principles of religiosity, economic development and sense gratification; by the three Vedas; made prosperous; the four classes of society; mostly brāhmaṇas; O King.
492.yw_zh: 在特瑞塔年代，人们献身于祭祀仪式和严苛苦行。他们不过于暴力或者说不贪求感官享乐。他们主要对宗教、经济发展和规范性的感官享乐感兴趣，他们遵守三部韦达的规范守则，从而生活殷实富庶。王啊，尽管在这个年代社会被划分为四个阶层，但人们大多是布茹阿玛纳。
492.yw_en: In the Tretā age people are devoted to ritual performances and severe austerities. They are not excessively violent or very lusty after sensual pleasure. Their interest lies primarily in religiosity, economic development and regulated sense gratification, and they achieve prosperity by following the prescriptions of the three Vedas. Although in this age society evolves into four separate classes, O King, most people are brāhmaṇas.

492.ldw:
tapaḥ-satya-dayā-dāneṣv
 ardhaṁ hrasvati dvāpare
hiṁsātuṣṭy-anṛta-dveṣair
 dharmasyādharma-lakṣaṇaiḥ|
492.ldw_fc: tapaḥ ; satya ; dayā ; dāneṣu ; ardham ; hrasvati ; dvāpare ; hiṁsā ; atuṣṭi ; anṛta ; dveṣaiḥ ; dharmasya ; adharma-lakṣaṇaiḥ
492.words_en_fc: of austerity; truth; mercy; and charity; one half; diminishes; in the age of Dvāpara; by violence; dissatisfaction; untruth; and hatred; of religion; by the qualities of irreligion.
492.yw_zh: 在杜瓦帕尔-尤嘎，苦行、真诚、仁慈和布施这些宗教品质减少一年，这是由于与之相反的反宗教品质——不满、虚伪、暴力和敌意所致。
492.yw_en: In Dvāpara-yuga the religious qualities of austerity, truth, mercy and charity are reduced to one half by their irreligious counterparts — dissatisfaction, untruth, violence and enmity.

492.ldw:
yaśasvino mahā-śīlāḥ
 svādhyāyādhyayane ratāḥ
āḍhyāḥ kuṭumbino hṛṣṭā
 varṇāḥ kṣatra-dvijottarāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yaśasvinaḥ ; mahā-śīlāḥ ; svādhyāya-adhyayane ; ratāḥ ; āḍhyāḥ ; kuṭumbinaḥ ; hṛṣṭāḥ ; varṇāḥ ; kṣatra-dvija-uttarāḥ
492.words_en_fc: eager for glory; noble; in study of the Vedic literature; absorbed; endowed with opulence; having large families; joyful; the four classes of society; represented mostly by the kṣatriyas and brāhmaṇas.
492.yw_zh: 在杜瓦帕尔年代，人们出生豪门望族，热衷于追求荣誉。他们致力于研究韦达经，拥有巨大的财富，维系庞大的家族，起劲享受生活。在四个阶层中，查锤亚和布茹阿玛纳为数最多。
492.yw_en: In the Dvāpara age people are interested in glory and are very noble. They devote themselves to the study of the Vedas, possess great opulence, support large families and enjoy life with vigor. Of the four classes, the kṣatriyas and brāhmaṇas are most numerous.

492.ldw:
kalau tu dharma-pādānāṁ
 turyāṁśo ’dharma-hetubhiḥ
edhamānaiḥ kṣīyamāṇo
 hy ante so ’pi vinaṅkṣyati|
492.ldw_fc: kalau ; tu ; dharma-pādānām ; turya-aṁśaḥ ; adharma ; hetubhiḥ ; edhamānaiḥ ; kṣīyamāṇaḥ ; hi ; ante ; saḥ ; api ; vinaṅkṣyati
492.words_en_fc: in the Age of Kali; and; of the legs of religion; one fourth; of irreligion; by the principles; which are increasing; decreasing; indeed; in the end; that one quarter; also; will be destroyed.
492.yw_zh: 在卡历年代，只剩下四分之一的宗教原则。就连这最后一点残余也将随着反宗教原则日渐增盛而继续衰减，最终将荡然无存。
492.yw_en: In the Age of Kali only one fourth of the religious principles remains. That last remnant will continuously be decreased by the ever-increasing principles of irreligion and will finally be destroyed.

492.ldw:
tasmin lubdhā durācārā
 nirdayāḥ śuṣka-vairiṇaḥ
durbhagā bhūri-tarṣāś ca
 śūdra-dāsottarāḥ prajāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: tasmin ; lubdhāḥ ; durācārāḥ ; nirdayāḥ ; śuṣka-vairiṇaḥ ; durbhagāḥ ; bhūri-tarṣāḥ ; ca ; śūdra-dāsa-uttarāḥ ; prajāḥ
492.words_en_fc: in that age; greedy; ill-behaved; merciless; prone to useless quarrel; unfortunate; obsessed by many kinds of hankering; and; predominantly low-class laborers and barbarians; the people.
492.yw_zh: 在卡历年代，人们贪婪成性，品行不良，无慈悲心，好无理取闹。卡历-尤嘎的人们物欲横流，十分不幸，几乎全是舒铎和野蛮人。
492.yw_en: In the Kali age people tend to be greedy, ill-behaved and merciless, and they fight one another without good reason. Unfortunate and obsessed with material desires, the people of Kali-yuga are almost all śūdras and barbarians.
492.yz_zh: 在这个年代，我们已经看到多数人是劳工、职员、渔夫、技工或其他舒铎行业的劳动者。神的进步奉献者和髙尚的政治领袖极度匮乏，就连独立经营的商人和农场主也越来越少，被企业集团并吞，变成卑躬屈膝的员工。地球大部分地区已经布满野蛮人和半野蛮人，使整个情形变得危险而阴暗。奎师那知觉运动被赋予力置，力挽狂澜，它是可怕的卡历-尤嘎的唯一希望。
492.yz_en: In this age, we can already observe that most people are laborers, clerks, fishermen, artisans or other kinds of workers within the śūdra category. Enlightened devotees of God and noble political leaders are extremely scarce, and even independent businessmen and farmers are a vanishing breed as huge business conglomerates increasingly convert them into subservient employees. Vast regions of the earth are already populated by barbarians and semibarbarous peoples, making the entire situation dangerous and bleak. The Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement is empowered to rectify the current dismal state of affairs. It is the only hope for the ghastly age called Kali-yuga.

492.ldw:
sattvaṁ rajas tama iti
 dṛśyante puruṣe guṇāḥ
kāla-sañcoditās te vai
 parivartanta ātmani|
492.ldw_fc: sattvam ; rajaḥ ; tamaḥ ; iti ; dṛśyante ; puruṣe ; guṇāḥ ; kāla-sañcoditāḥ ; te ; vai ; parivartante ; ātmani
492.words_en_fc: goodness; passion; ignorance; thus; are seen; in a person; the modes of material nature; impelled by time; they; indeed; undergo permutation; within the mind.
492.yw_zh: 物质属性——善良、激情和愚昧属性——互相角逐，反映在人的心意里，它们在时间力量的推动下运转着。
492.yw_en: The material modes — goodness, passion and ignorance — whose permutations are observed within a person’s mind, are set into motion by the power of time.
492.yz_zh: 这些诗节所描述的四个年代全是各种物质自然属性的展示。真诚年代，萨提亚-尤嘎，明显展示的是物质善良属性，而卡历-尤嘎展示出愚昧属性占据主导。据施瑞拉•维施瓦纳塔•恰夸瓦尔提•塔库尔说，在每个年代中，其他三个年代偶尔也略有展示。因此，即便在萨提亚-尤嘎，也有处在愚昧属性中的恶魔显现于世，在卡历-尤嘎，最高的宗教原则也会兴盛一段时间。正如《圣典博伽瓦谭》所述，自然三属性遍存万有，不过主导属性决定一切物质现象的普遍特点。所以，在毎个年代，三属性所占比例各不相同。在代表善良属性的萨提亚年代，代表激情属性的特瑞塔年代，代表激情与愚昧属性的杜瓦帕尔年代或代表愚昧属性的卡历年代中，其他三个年代以次因素的形式存在。
492.yz_en: The four ages described in these verses are manifestations of various modes of material nature. The age of truth, Satya-yuga, manifests the predominance of material goodness, and Kali-yuga manifests the predominance of ignorance. According to Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura, within each age the other three ages occasionally manifest as sub-ages. Thus even within Satya-yuga a demon in the mode of ignorance may appear, and within the Age of Kali the highest religious principles may flourish for some time. As described in Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, the three modes of nature are present everywhere and in everything, but the predominant mode, or combination of modes, determines the general character of any material phenomenon. In each age, therefore, the three modes are present in varying proportions. The particular age represented by goodness (Satya), passion (Tretā), passion and ignorance (Dvāpara) or ignorance (Kali) exists within each of the other ages as a subfactor.

492.ldw:
prabhavanti yadā sattve
 mano-buddhīndriyāṇi ca
tadā kṛta-yugaṁ vidyāj
 jñāne tapasi yad ruciḥ|
492.ldw_fc: prabhavanti ; yadā ; sattve ; manaḥ ; buddhi ; indriyāṇi ; ca ; tadā ; kṛta-yugam ; vidyāt ; jñāne ; tapasi ; yat ; ruciḥ
492.words_en_fc: they are predominantly manifest; when; in the mode of goodness; the mind; intelligence; senses; and; then; the age of Kṛta; should be understood; in knowledge; and austerity; when; pleasure.
492.yw_zh: 当心意、智性和感官牢固地处在善良属性中，要知道，那个时候便是萨提亚-尤嘎，真诚年代。那时，人们从知识和苦行中获得快乐。
492.yw_en: When the mind, intelligence and senses are solidly fixed in the mode of goodness, that time should be understood as Satya-yuga, the age of truth. People then take pleasure in knowledge and austerity.
492.yz_zh: Krta—词的意思是“从事”或“执行。”因此，在真诚年代，一切宗教职责均得以恰当履行，人们从灵性知识和苦行中获得巨大的喜乐。甚至在卡历-尤嘎，稳处于善良属性中的人也乐于培养灵性知识和从事规范苦行。已征服色欲的人，才能稳处在这种祟高而喜乐的境界中。
492.yz_en: The word kṛta means “performed” or “executed.” Thus in the age of truth all religious duties are duly performed, and people take great pleasure in spiritual knowledge and austerity. Even in the Kali-yuga, those who are situated in the mode of goodness take pleasure in the cultivation of spiritual knowledge and the regulated performance of austerity. This sublime state of existence is possible for one who has conquered sex desire.

492.ldw:
yadā karmasu kāmyeṣu
 bhaktir yaśasi dehinām
tadā tretā rajo-vṛttir
 iti jānīhi buddhiman|
492.ldw_fc: yadā ; karmasu ; kāmyeṣu ; bhaktiḥ ; yaśasi ; dehinām ; tadā ; tretā ; rajaḥ-vṛttiḥ ; iti ; jānīhi ; buddhi-man
492.words_en_fc: when; in duties; based on selfish desire; devotion; in honor; of the embodied souls; then; the age of Tretā; predominated by activities in the mode of passion; thus; you should understand; O intelligent King Parīkṣit.
492.yw_zh: 最具智慧的人啊，受制约的灵魂投入地履行各自的职责时，却别有用心，寻求个人名望，你要明白，特瑞塔年代就是这种情况，在特瑞塔年代，激情型活动尤为突出。
492.yw_en: O most intelligent one, when the conditioned souls are devoted to their duties but have ulterior motives and seek personal prestige, you should understand such a situation to be the age of Tretā, in which the functions of passion are prominent.

492.ldw:
yadā lobhas tv asantoṣo
 māno dambho ’tha matsaraḥ
karmaṇāṁ cāpi kāmyānāṁ
 dvāparaṁ tad rajas-tamaḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yadā ; lobhaḥ ; tu ; asantoṣaḥ ; mānaḥ ; dambhaḥ ; atha ; matsaraḥ ; karmaṇām ; ca ; api ; kāmyānām ; dvāparam ; tat ; rajaḥ-tamaḥ
492.words_en_fc: when; greed; indeed; dissatisfaction; false pride; hypocrisy; and; envy; of activities; and; also; selfish; the age of Dvāpara; that; predominated by a mixture of the modes of passion and ignorance.
492.yw_zh: 当贪婪、不满、虚假的骄傲、伪善、嫉妒日渐显著，人们越来越被自私的活动吸引，那时便是杜瓦帕尔年代，这个年代为激情和愚昧的混合属性所主宰。
492.yw_en: When greed, dissatisfaction, false pride, hypocrisy and envy become prominent, along with attraction for selfish activities, such a time is the age of Dvāpara, dominated by the mixed modes of passion and ignorance.

492.ldw:
yadā māyānṛtaṁ tandrā
 nidrā hiṁsā viṣādanam
śoka-mohau bhayaṁ dainyaṁ
 sa kalis tāmasaḥ smṛtaḥ|
492.ldw_fc: yadā ; māyā ; anṛtam ; tandrā ; nidrā ; hiṁsā ; viṣādanam ; śoka ; mohau ; bhayam ; dainyam ; saḥ ; kaliḥ ; tāmasaḥ ; smṛtaḥ
492.words_en_fc: when; deceit; false speech; sloth; sleep and intoxication; violence; depression; lamentation; and delusion; fear; poverty; that; the Age of Kali; in the mode of ignorance; is considered.
492.yw_zh: 当欺骗、说谎、懒惰、昏睡、暴力、消沉、悲伤、迷惑、恐惧和贫穷的现显著时，便是深处愚昧属性的卡历年代。
492.yw_en: When there is a predominance of cheating, lying, sloth, sleepiness, violence, depression, lamentation, bewilderment, fear and poverty, that age is Kali, the age of the mode of ignorance.
492.yz_zh: 在卡历•尤嘎，人们几乎一昧地沉醉于粗糙的物质追求，对觉悟自我索然无味。
492.yz_en: In Kali-yuga, people are almost exclusively devoted to gross materialism, with hardly any affinity for self-realization.

492.ldw:
tasmāt kṣudra-dṛśo martyāḥ
 kṣudra-bhāgyā mahāśanāḥ
kāmino vitta-hīnāś ca
 svairiṇyaś ca striyo ’satīḥ|
492.ldw_fc: tasmāt ; kṣudra-dṛśaḥ ; martyāḥ ; kṣudra-bhāgyāḥ ; mahā-aśanāḥ ; kāminaḥ ; vitta-hīnāḥ ; ca ; svairiṇyaḥ ; ca ; striyaḥ ; asatīḥ
492.words_en_fc: due to these qualities of the Age of Kali; shortsighted; human beings; unfortunate; excessive in their eating habits; full of lust; lacking wealth; and; independent in their social dealings; and; the women; unchaste.
492.yw_zh: 由于卡历年代的恶劣品质，人类将变得毫无远见，十分不幸，贪食贪饮，色欲冲天，穷困潦倒。妇女变得不守 操，自由结交男性，朝三暮四。
492.yw_en: Because of the bad qualities of the Age of Kali, human beings will become shortsighted, unfortunate, gluttonous, lustful and poverty-stricken. The women, becoming unchaste, will freely wander from one man to the next.
492.yz_zh: 在卡历年代，某些伪知识分子支持乱性，寻求个体自由。实际上，把自我认同于躯体，追求躯体上而非灵魂上的“个体自由”，这全是愚昧之极，色欲的奴隶的征候。妇女不守贞洁，致使许多孩子是婚外子，成为色欲的产物。这些孩子在心理不健康的环境中成长，招致愚昧而神经不健全的社会产生。这些现象己遍及全球。
492.yz_en: In the Age of Kali certain pseudointellectuals, seeking individual freedom, support sexual promiscuity. In fact, identification of the self with the body and the pursuit of “individual freedom” in the body rather than in the soul are signs of the most dismal ignorance and slavery to lust. When women are unchaste, many children are born out of wedlock as products of lust. These children grow up in psychologically unfavorable circumstances, and a neurotic, ignorant society arises. Symptoms of this are already manifest throughout the world.

492.ldw:
dasyūtkṛṣṭā janapadā
 vedāḥ pāṣaṇḍa-dūṣitāḥ
rājānaś ca prajā-bhakṣāḥ
 śiśnodara-parā dvijāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: dasyu-utkṛṣṭāḥ ; jana-padāḥ ; vedāḥ ; pāṣaṇḍa ; dūṣitāḥ ; rājānaḥ ; ca ; prajā-bhakṣāḥ ; śiśna-udara ; parāḥ ; dvijāḥ
492.words_en_fc: predominated by thieves; the populated places; the Vedic scriptures; by atheists; contaminated; the political leaders; and; consuming the populace; to the genitals and belly; dedicated; the brāhmaṇas.
492.yw_zh: 城市将被盗贼掌控，韦达经将受到无神论者推敲曲解的玷污，政治首脑们将几乎是剥削民众，所谓的祭司和知识分子将是他们的肚腹和生殖器的奉献者。
492.yw_en: Cities will be dominated by thieves, the Vedas will be contaminated by speculative interpretations of atheists, political leaders will virtually consume the citizens, and the so-called priests and intellectuals will be devotees of their bellies and genitals.
492.yz_zh:
许多大城市在夜间是不安全的。例如，众所周知，没有一个神智健全的人会在夜晚到纽约中心公园散步，因为他知道，如果那样做肯定遭抢劫。在这个年代，大城市除了盗贼猖嫌之外，还充满着残酷的生意人，他们热情地说服人们购买消费一些毫无价值甚至有害的产品。文档记载得很淸楚，牛肉、香烟、酒类和许多其他现代产品毁人健康，更不消说心理健康了，然而，当代资本主义者毫不犹豫地在书中使用种种心理技巧，说服人们消费这些东西。现代城市到处是心理和环埦污染，甚至普通大众都发现这些东西令人不堪忍受。

这节诗还指出，在这个年代，韦达经典的教导将被歪曲。各大学教授的印度教课程把印度宗教说成是多神教，并引人得到非人格解脱，尽管有无数证据恰恰与之相反。事实上，所有韦达典籍是—个统—的整体，正如主奎师那本人在（博伽梵歌原意》中说：vcdais ca sarvair aham eva vedyah“全部节达经旨在了解我【奎师那】。”一切韦达典籍的目的是让我们了解至尊人格绝对真理一维施努或奎师那。虽然神以许多名字著称，以许多形体显现。但神是一个绝对存在，并且他是一个人。然而，这真正的韦达知识在卡历-尤嘎被隐藏起来。

在这节诗，舒卡戴瓦敏锐地指出“政治首脑们将几乎是剥削民众，所谓的祭司和知识分子将是他们的肚腹和生殖器的奉献者。”这真是一语中的。
492.yz_en:
Many large cities are unsafe at night. For example, it is understood that no sane person will walk in New York’s Central Park at night because he knows he will almost certainly be mugged. Apart from ordinary thieves, who abound in this age, large cities are filled with cutthroat businessmen, who enthusiastically convince people to purchase and consume useless or even harmful products. It has been well documented that beef, tobacco, liquor and many other modern products destroy one’s physical health, what to speak of mental health, and yet modern capitalists do not hesitate to use every psychological trick in the book to convince people to consume these things. Modern cities are full of mental and atmospheric pollution, and even ordinary citizens are finding them unbearable.

This verse also points out that the teachings of the Vedic scriptures will be distorted in this age. Great universities teach courses on Hinduism in which Indian religion, despite limitless evidence to the contrary, is described as polytheistic and leading to an impersonal salvation. In fact, all Vedic literature is a unified whole, as stated by Lord Kṛṣṇa Himself in Bhagavad-gītā (15.15): vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyaḥ. “By all the Vedas I [Kṛṣṇa] am to be known.” All Vedic literature is meant for enlightening us about the Supreme Personal Absolute Truth — Viṣṇu, or Kṛṣṇa. Although known by many names and appearing in many forms, God is a single absolute entity, and He is a person. But this true Vedic understanding is hidden in the Kali-yuga.

In this verse Śukadeva astutely observes that “political leaders will virtually consume the citizens, and the so-called priests and intellectuals will be devotees of their bellies and genitals.” How sadly true this statement is.

492.ldw:
avratā baṭavo ’śaucā
 bhikṣavaś ca kuṭumbinaḥ
tapasvino grāma-vāsā
 nyāsino ’tyartha-lolupāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: avratāḥ ; baṭavaḥ ; aśaucāḥ ; bhikṣavaḥ ; ca ; kuṭumbinaḥ ; tapasvinaḥ ; grāma-vāsāḥ ; nyāsinaḥ ; atyartha-lolupāḥ
492.words_en_fc: failing to execute their vows; the brahmacārīs; unclean; prone to begging; and; the householders; those who have gone to the forest for austerities; village residents; the sannyāsīs; excessively greedy for wealth.
492.yw_zh: 布茹阿玛查瑞们将不履行他们的誓言，普遍变得不洁净，行为不检点，居士们将逐渐成为乞丐，瓦纳帕斯塔们将居于村落，而桑尼亚西们会变得贪财。
492.yw_en: The brahmacārīs will fail to execute their vows and become generally unclean, the householders will become beggars, the vānaprasthas will live in the villages, and the sannyāsīs will become greedy for wealth.
492.yz_zh:
Brahmacarya布茹阿玛査亚，贞守生生活，在卡历年代实际己不存在。在美国，许多男子学校己变成男女合校，因为男生公然决绝不近女色的生活。而且，众所周知，在整个西方国家，学生公寓是世上最脏的地方，正如这里的asaucah—词所预言的。

关于居士乞丐这一点，当主的奉献者挨门逐户派发超然典籍，请求布施用于传播神的荣耀时，不耐烦的居士们通常回答说，“应该给我布施才好。”在卡历-尤嘎，居士们不乐善好施。恰恰相反，他们格外吝啬，当灵性僧侣光临家宅，他们却面生怒色。

按照韦达文化，人过半百就该退隐，前往圣地苦修，以臻至灵性完美。然而，在像美国这样的国家已建造了退休城，老年人住在那里浪费余生，愚弄自己，他们打高尔夫球、兵乓球，玩转盘游戏，还谈情说爱。尽管他们己老态龙钟。如此毫无顾忌地滥用余生表明他们坚决不想了解人生的真谛，这无疑是对神的不敬。

词语nyasino tyartha-lolupah说明富有魅力的宗教领袖，即便是那些不具魅力的人，将自称是先知先觉者、圣人或化身，欺骗无知大众，中饱私禳。因此，国际奎师那知觉协会在努力确立真正的贞守生生活，虔诚的居士生活，庄严而进步的退隐生活，并为世人树立真正的灵性领袖风范。今天，1982年5月9月，在巴西里约热内卢这个花花世界里，我们授予三位年轻人，两个巴西人，一个美国人，进入桑尼亚西，生命弃绝晋阶，衷心希望他们忠诚不渝地执行弃绝生涯的严格誓言，在南美洲树立可靠的灵性领袖榜样。
492.yz_en:
Brahmacarya, celibate student life, is practically nonexistent in the Age of Kali. In America, many boys’ schools have become coeducational because young men frankly refuse to live without the constant companionship of lusty young girls. Also, we have personally observed throughout the Western world that student residences are among the dirtiest places on earth, as predicted here by the word aśaucāḥ.

Concerning householder beggars, when devotees of the Lord go door to door distributing transcendental literature and requesting donations for the propagation of God’s glories, irritated householders commonly reply, “Someone should give me a donation.” Householders in Kali-yuga are not charitable. Instead, because of their miserly mentality, they become irritated when spiritual mendicants approach them.

In Vedic culture, at the age of fifty, couples retire to sacred places for austere life and spiritual perfection. In countries like America, however, retirement cities have been constructed wherein elderly people can make fools of themselves by wasting the last years of their lives playing golf, ping-pong and shuffleboard and by engaging in pathetic attempts at love affairs even while their bodies are horribly rotting and their minds are growing senile. This shameless abuse of the venerable last years of life denotes a stubborn unwillingness to acknowledge the actual purpose of human life and is certainly an offense against God.

The words nyāsino ’tyartha-lolupāḥ indicate that charismatic religious leaders, and even those who are not charismatic, will proclaim themselves prophets, saints and incarnations to cheat the innocent public and fatten their bank accounts. Therefore the International Society for Krishna Consciousness is working arduously to establish bona fide celibate student life, religious householder life, dignified and progressive retirement, and genuine spiritual leadership for the entire world. Today, May 9, 1982, in the sensual city of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, we have awarded sannyāsa, the renounced order of life, to three young men, two Brazilians and one American, with the sincere hope that they will faithfully execute the rigid vows of renounced life and provide authentic spiritual leadership in South America.

492.ldw:
hrasva-kāyā mahāhārā
 bhūry-apatyā gata-hriyaḥ
śaśvat kaṭuka-bhāṣiṇyaś
 caurya-māyoru-sāhasāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: hrasva-kāyāḥ ; mahā-āhārāḥ ; bhūri-apatyāḥ ; gata-hriyaḥ ; śaśvat ; kaṭuka ; bhāṣiṇyaḥ ; caurya ; māyā ; uru-sāhasāḥ
492.words_en_fc: having dwarfed bodies; eating too much; having many children; losing their shyness; constantly; harshly; speaking; exhibiting the tendencies of thievery; deceit; and great audacity.
492.yw_zh: 女人将变得身材矮小，吃得太多，生的孩子多得自己没法好好照顾，她们还将丧失一切羞涩。她们将总是言语刻薄，行窃偷盗，讹诈欺骗，厚颜无耻。
492.yw_en: Women will become much smaller in size, and they will eat too much, have more children than they can properly take care of, and lose all shyness. They will always speak harshly and will exhibit qualities of thievery, deceit and unrestrained audacity.

492.ldw:
paṇayiṣyanti vai kṣudrāḥ
 kirāṭāḥ kūṭa-kāriṇaḥ
anāpady api maṁsyante
 vārtāṁ sādhu jugupsitām|
492.ldw_fc: paṇayiṣyanti ; vai ; kṣudrāḥ ; kirāṭāḥ ; kūṭa-kāriṇaḥ ; anāpadi ; api ; maṁsyante ; vārtām ; sādhu ; jugupsitām
492.words_en_fc: will engage in commerce; indeed; petty; the merchants; indulging in cheating; when there is no emergency; even; people will consider; an occupation; good; which is actually contemptible.
492.yw_zh: 商人将从事小生意，靠欺骗赚钱。即便在非紧急时候，人们也会认为任何堕落的职业都是完全可以接受的。
492.yw_en: Businessmen will engage in petty commerce and earn their money by cheating. Even when there is no emergency, people will consider any degraded occupation quite acceptable.
492.yz_zh: 尽管有其他职业可供选择，但人们仍毫不犹豫地选择在煤矿、屠宰场、钢厂、沙漠、浮动石油钻井平台、潜艇和其他恶劣环境中的工作。也正如这里说的，商人将认为欺骗说谎是做生意的最体面的方式。这些全是卡历年代的征兆。
492.yz_en: Although other occupations are available, people do not hesitate to work in coal mines, slaughterhouses, steel mills, deserts, floating oil rigs, submarines and other equally abominable situations. As also mentioned here, businessmen will consider cheating and lying to be a perfectly respectable way to do business. These are all symptoms of the Age of Kali.

492.ldw:
patiṁ tyakṣyanti nirdravyaṁ
 bhṛtyā apy akhilottamam
bhṛtyaṁ vipannaṁ patayaḥ
 kaulaṁ gāś cāpayasvinīḥ|
492.ldw_fc: patim ; tyakṣyanti ; nirdravyam ; bhṛtyāḥ ; api ; akhila-uttamam ; bhṛtyam ; vipannam ; patayaḥ ; kaulam ; gāḥ ; ca ; apayasvinīḥ
492.words_en_fc: a master; they will abandon; lacking property; servants; even; most excellent in personal qualities; a servant; incapacitated; masters; belonging to the family for generations; cows; and; which have stopped giving milk.
492.yw_zh: 仆人会离弃丧失财富的主人，即便主人是一位具有模范品德的圣人。主人将抛弃力不从心的侍从，即使仆人已侍奉过家族世代。乳牛不再产奶时，将遭到遗弃或宰杀。
492.yw_en: Servants will abandon a master who has lost his wealth, even if that master is a saintly person of exemplary character. Masters will abandon an incapacitated servant, even if that servant has been in the family for generations. Cows will be abandoned or killed when they stop giving milk.
492.yz_zh: 在印度，乳牛被认为是神圣的，这不是因为印度人是神话图腾的崇拜者，而是由于印度人深知乳牛乃是母亲。就像孩子靠喝牛奶长大，几乎所有人都是如此，因此，乳牛是我们的母亲之一。毫无疑问，母亲是神圣的，所以我们不该宰杀神圣的乳牛。
492.yz_en: In India, the cow is considered sacred not because Indian people are primitive worshipers of mythological totems but because Hindus intelligently understand that the cow is a mother. As children, nearly all of us were nourished with cow’s milk, and therefore the cow is one of our mothers. Certainly one’s mother is sacred, and therefore we should not kill the sacred cow.

492.ldw:
pitṛ-bhrātṛ-suhṛj-jñātīn
 hitvā saurata-sauhṛdāḥ
nanāndṛ-śyāla-saṁvādā
 dīnāḥ straiṇāḥ kalau narāḥ|
492.ldw_fc: pitṛ ; bhrātṛ ; suhṛt ; jñātīn ; hitvā ; saurata ; sauhṛdāḥ ; nanāndṛ ; śyāla ; saṁvādāḥ ; dīnāḥ ; straiṇāḥ ; kalau ; narāḥ
492.words_en_fc: their fathers; brothers; well-wishing friends; and immediate relatives; giving up; based on sexual relationships; their conception of friendship; with their wives’ sisters; and wives’ brothers; associating regularly; wretched; effeminate; in Kali-yuga; the men.
492.yw_zh: 在卡历-尤嘎，男人很不幸，受女人摆布。他们将冷落他们的父辈、兄弟和其他亲朋好友，反而亲近他们的妻子的兄弟姐妹。如此，他们关于友情的概念将纯粹基于性关系。
492.yw_en: In Kali-yuga men will be wretched and controlled by women. They will reject their fathers, brothers, other relatives and friends and will instead associate with the sisters and brothers of their wives. Thus their conception of friendship will be based exclusively on sexual ties.

492.ldw:
śūdrāḥ pratigrahīṣyanti
 tapo-veṣopajīvinaḥ
dharmaṁ vakṣyanty adharma-jñā
 adhiruhyottamāsanam|
492.ldw_fc: śūdrāḥ ; pratigrahīṣyanti ; tapaḥ ; veṣa ; upajīvinaḥ ; dharmam ; vakṣyanti ; adharma-jñāḥ ; adhiruhya ; uttama-āsanam
492.words_en_fc: lowly, common workers; will accept religious charity; by shows of austerity; and by dressing as mendicants; earning their living; the principles of religion; will speak about; those who know nothing about religion; mounting; a high seat.
492.yw_zh: 无教养的人会代表主接受布施，靠假装苦行，穿僧人装束谋生。那些昧于宗教的人将荣登高位，装模作样地畅谈宗教原则。
492.yw_en: Uncultured men will accept charity on behalf of the Lord and will earn their livelihood by making a show of austerity and wearing a mendicant’s dress. Those who know nothing about religion will mount a high seat and presume to speak on religious principles.
492.yz_zh: 这里明确说明，冒牌古茹、斯瓦米、祭司等将四处泛滥。
492.yz_en: The epidemic of bogus gurus, swamis, priests and so forth is explicitly described here.

492.ldw:
nityam udvigna-manaso
 durbhikṣa-kara-karśitāḥ
niranne bhū-tale rājan
 anāvṛṣṭi-bhayāturāḥ|vāso-’nna-pāna-śayana-
 vyavāya-snāna-bhūṣaṇaiḥ
hīnāḥ piśāca-sandarśā
 bhaviṣyanti kalau prajāḥ|

492.words_en_fc: constantly; agitated; their minds; by famine; and taxes; emaciated; when there is no food to be found; upon the surface of the earth; O King Parīkṣit; of drought; because of fear; anxious; clothing; food; drink; rest; sex; bathing; and personal ornaments; lacking; appearing just like ghostly demons; they will become; in the Age of Kali; the people.
492.yw_zh: 在止历年代，人们的心意总是焦虑不安。我亲爱的王，他们将被饥荒和税收弄得身心交瘁，时刻惴惴不安，害怕遭遇干旱。他们将缺衣少食。休息不好，丧失性能力，不能正常沐浴，也没有饰品装扮身体。实际上，卡历-尤嘎的人们将逐渐变得像鬼魂附体的幽灵一样。
492.yw_en: In the Age of Kali, people’s minds will always be agitated. They will become emaciated by famine and taxation, my dear King, and will always be disturbed by fear of drought. They will lack adequate clothing, food and drink, will be unable to properly rest, have sex or bathe themselves, and will have no ornaments to decorate their bodies. In fact, the people of Kali-yuga will gradually come to appear like ghostly, haunted creatures.
492.yz_zh: 这里描写的征兆已经在许多国家十分普遍，并将逐渐蔓延到为不虔诚活动和物质主义所吞噬的其他地方。
492.yz_en: The symptoms described here are already prevalent in many countries of the world and will gradually spread to other places engulfed by impiety and materialism.

492.ldw:
kalau kākiṇike ’py arthe
 vigṛhya tyakta-sauhṛdāḥ
tyakṣyanti ca priyān prāṇān
 haniṣyanti svakān api|

492.yw_zh: 在卡历-尤嘎，人们将为了几枚硬币而彼此仇恨。他们会抛开一切友情，甘愿丧失自己的性命，甚至杀死他们的亲人。
492.yw_en: In Kali-yuga men will develop hatred for each other even over a few coins. Giving up all friendly relations, they will be ready to lose their own lives and kill even their own relatives.

492.yw_zh: 男人将不再保护他们年迈的双亲，他们的孩子和他们可敬的妻子。他们将极其堕落，只关心满足他们自己的肚腹和生殖器。
492.yw_en: Men will no longer protect their elderly parents, their children or their respectable wives. Thoroughly degraded, they will care only to satisfy their own bellies and genitals.
492.yz_zh:
在这个年代，尽管年迈的父母花了毕生精力照顾孩子，但仍有许多人已把他们的父母送进寂寞孤独、光怪陆离的养老院。

在这个年代，儿童还受到各种虐待。近年，儿童自杀事件急剧增加，因为生育他们的父母不是满怀爱与虔诚之心，而是堕落自私的人。实际上，婴儿经常是在大人服用过节育丸、避孕药或采取其他人为避孕措施之后诞生。在这种情况下，当今父母很难在德育方面指导他们的孩子。通常，由于父母昧于灵性科学，所以不能引导他们的孩子走上解脱之途，故而没有尽到家居生活的首要职责。

正如这节诗预言的，通奸已成为平常事，普通大众极其关心吃和性生活，他们认为这比了解绝对真理重要得多。
492.yz_en:
In this age many people are already sending their elderly parents away to lonely, and often bizarre, old-age homes, although the elderly parents spent their entire lives serving their children.

Young children are also tormented in many ways in this age. Suicide among children has increased dramatically in recent years because they are being born not to loving, religious parents but to degraded, selfish men and women. In fact, children are often born because a birth-control pill, a prophylactic or some other contraceptive device malfunctioned. Under such conditions, it is very difficult nowadays for parents to morally guide their children. Generally ignorant of spiritual science, parents cannot lead their children on the path of liberation and thus fail to fulfill their primary responsibility in family life.

As predicted in this verse, adultery has become common, and people in general are extremely concerned with eating and sex, which they consider far more important than knowing the Absolute Truth.

492.ldw:
kalau na rājan jagatāṁ paraṁ guruṁ
 tri-loka-nāthānata-pāda-paṅkajam
prāyeṇa martyā bhagavantam acyutaṁ
 yakṣyanti pāṣaṇḍa-vibhinna-cetasaḥ|

492.words_en_fc: in the Age of Kali; not; O King; of the universe; the supreme; spiritual master; of the three worlds; by the various masters; bowed down to; whose lotus feet; for the most part; human beings; the Personality of Godhead; Lord Acyuta; they will offer sacrifice; by atheism; diverted; their intelligence.
492.yw_zh: 王啊， 在卡历年代，人们的智性会转向无神论，他们将几乎再也不向至尊人格首神，宇宙的至尊灵性导师，做献祭。虽然掌控三个世界的伟大人物们都向至尊的莲花足顶礼膜拜，但这个年代渺小而悲惨的人类却不会这么做。
492.yw_en: O King, in the Age of Kali people’s intelligence will be diverted by atheism, and they will almost never offer sacrifice to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the supreme spiritual master of the universe. Although the great personalities who control the three worlds all bow down to the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord, the petty and miserable human beings of this age will not do so.
492.yz_zh:
自太古以来，想要找到一切存在之源绝对真理的动力，曾驱策许多哲学家、神学家和其他持不同信仰的智者时至今日，依然如此。然而，经过清醒地分析形形色色的所谓哲学、宗教、道路、生活方式等，我们发现几乎在所有这些情况中，终极目标全是非人格或无形的、而这种对绝对真理的非人格或无形的概念有其严重的逻辑缺陷。按照一般的逻辑规则，一种结果应该直接或间接地体现出其原因的属性或本性。因此，缺乏人格性和活动的那个无法是一切人格性和活动的渊源。

我们探究终极真理的不可抑制的傾向通过从哲学、科学和神秘主义方面去探索万物之本源而表现出来。这看似因果互相交织的无限罗网的物质世界决非绝对真理，因为对物质元素的科学观察说明，这个世界的原料，物质能量，在无休止地转化成各种境况和形状。因此，某种物质现实无法是其他一切的终极源头。

我们或许推测某种或其他形状的物质曾一度存在。然而，这种理论对于诸如麻省理工大学的当代宇宙学家来说不再有吸引力。即便我们断定物质曾一直存在，但若想满足探索绝对真理的哲学催涌，就要解释知觉的根源。虽然当代经验主义狂热分子声称除了物质没有什么是真实的，但人人都切身体会到知觉决非石头、铅笔或水一样的物质。知觉本身有别于知觉的对象，不是物质的，而是感知和理解的过程。虽然有大量证据表明物质与知觉之间系统地相互依存的关系，但没有任何严谨的经验性证据说明物质是知觉的原因。所以，物质世界曾一直存在并因此是终极真理的理论没有科学地或甚至直观地解释知觉的根源，而知觉乃是我们存在最根本最真实的方面。

而且，正如宾厄姆顿纽约州立大学的理査德•汤姆逊博士论证的，也正如几位称赞他的著作的诺贝尔物理学奖获得者所确证的，掌控物质转化的自然法则根本没有涵盖充足的综合信息以说明发生在我们自身体内和其他生命形体内复杂得令人难以置信的事情。换言之，不仅物质自然法则没有说明知觉的存在，甚至还不能以复杂的有机物为例解释物质元素之间的相互作用。就连西方第一位了不起的哲学家苏格拉底，他对试图以机械论原理确立终极因果关系都感到厌恶。

太阳光的热度和亮度足以让任何一个理性的人确信，太阳，光源，绝不是一个漆黑寒冷的球体，而是充满无限光与热的储存所。同样，创造之中无数人格和个体知觉的例子，足以表明存在着知觉与个人行为的无尽源泉。在希腊哲学家柏拉图的对话录《斐利布斯篇》Philebus中，他主张说，就像我们体内的物质元素源自宇宙中物质元素的浩瀚无垠的储藏库，我们的理性智慧也源自于一个存在于宇宙中伟大的宇宙智慧，而这至高无上的智慧便是神，创造者。不幸的是，在卡历-尤嘎，许多重要思想家不能明白这一点，反而否认我们个人知觉之源，绝对真理，具有知觉和人格性。这等于说太阳既冷又黑一样。

在卡历-尤嘎，许多人以千篇一律的陈词滥调争论说“如果神有形体或具有人格，他岂不是有限的。”在这种不符逻辑的言辞中，一个合格的术语被错误地呈现在普遍意义中，其实应该这么说，“如果神有像我们所经历的物质躯体或物质人格，他便是有限的。”可是，我们省略了形容词‘物质的’，给出一个错误的断言，似乎我们充分了解整个现实中的躯体与人格。

《博伽梵歌原意》，《圣典博伽瓦谭》和其他韦达典籍教导说，绝对真理的超然形体与人格无极无限。明确地说，真正无限的神不仅要在量上，而且还要在质上是无限的，不幸的是，在我们当今机械工业年代，我们倾向于只在量上定义无限，所以我们没有留意无限的人格性乃是无穷无尽者不可或缺的方面。也就是说，神必须拥有无限的美丽，无限的富裕，无限的智慧，无限的幽默，无限的慈爱，无限的愤怒等等。无限是绝对的，如果我们在这世界所看到的一切不涵盖在我们对绝对的概念中，那么这个概念是针对有限之物而言，决非指绝对者。

只在卡历-尤嘎才有哲学家愚蠢到傲慢地以唯物论的相对方式定义万事万物的至尊绝对者一神，吹嘘自己是觉悟的思想家的地步。无论我们的大脑有多么超级，都该具备常识，将其置于至尊人格首神的足下。
492.yz_en:
The impulse to find the Absolute Truth, the source of all existence, has motivated philosophers, theologians and other intellectuals of various persuasions since time immemorial, and continues to do so today. However, soberly analyzing the ever-increasing plurality of so-called philosophies, religions, paths, ways of life and so on, we find that in almost all cases the ultimate objective is something impersonal or formless. But this idea of an impersonal or formless Absolute Truth has serious logical flaws. According to ordinary rules of logic, a particular effect should directly or indirectly embody the attributes, or nature, of its own cause. Thus that which has no personality or activity could hardly be the source of all personality and all activity.

Our irrepressible proclivity to philosophize about the ultimate truth often expresses itself through philosophical, scientific and mystical attempts to discover that from which everything emanates. This material world, which is a seemingly limitless network of interactive causes and effects, is certainly not the Absolute Truth, since scientific observation of material elements indicates that the stuff of this world, material energy, is endlessly transformed into different states and shapes. Therefore, one particular instance of material reality cannot be the ultimate source of all other things.

We may speculate that matter in some shape or other has always existed. This theory, however, is no longer attractive to modern cosmologists, such as those at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. And even if we do posit that matter has always existed, we still must explain the source of consciousness if we want to satisfy our philosophical impulse toward discovering the Absolute Truth. Although modern empirical fanatics state that nothing is real except matter, everyone commonly experiences that consciousness is not the same kind of substance as a stone, a pencil or water. Awareness itself, in contradistinction to the objects of awareness, is not a physical entity but rather a process of perception and understanding. While there is ample evidence of a systematic interdependent relationship between matter and consciousness, there is no rigid empirical evidence whatsoever that matter is the cause of consciousness. Thus the theory that the material world has always existed and is therefore the ultimate truth does not scientifically or even intuitively explain the source of consciousness, which is the most fundamentally real aspect of our existence.

Furthermore, as demonstrated by Dr. Richard Thompson of the State University of New York at Binghamton and confirmed by several Nobel laureates in physics who have praised his work, the laws of nature governing the transformation of matter simply do not contain sufficiently complex information to account for the inconceivable complexity of events taking place within our own bodies and those of other life forms. In other words, not only do the material laws of nature fail to account for the existence of consciousness, but they cannot explain even the interaction of material elements at complex organic levels. Even Socrates, the first great Western philosopher, was disgusted with the attempt to establish ultimate causality in terms of mechanistic principles.

The heat and luminosity of the sun’s rays demonstrate to the satisfaction of any rational man that the sun, the source of the rays, is certainly not a dark, cold globe but rather a reservoir of almost unlimited heat and light. Similarly, the innumerable instances of personality and personal consciousness within creation are more than adequate to demonstrate the existence, somewhere, of an unlimited reservoir of consciousness and personal behavior. In his dialogue Philebus, the Greek philosopher Plato argued that just as the material elements in our body are derived from a vast reservoir of material elements existing within the universe, our rational intelligence is also derived from a great cosmic intelligence existing within the universe, and this supreme intelligence is God, the creator. Unfortunately, in Kali-yuga many leading thinkers cannot understand this and instead deny that the Absolute Truth, the source of our personal consciousness, has consciousness and personality. This is as reasonable as saying that the sun is cold and dark.

In Kali-yuga, many people present cheap, stereotyped arguments, such as “If God had a body or personality, He would be limited.” In this inadequate attempt at logic, a qualified term is falsely presented in a universal sense. What really should be said is, “If God had a material body or a material personality like those we have experienced, He would be limited.” But we leave out the qualifying adjective material and make a pseudouniversal assertion, as if we understood the full range, within total reality, of bodies and personality.

Bhagavad-gītā, Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam and other Vedic literatures teach that the transcendental form and personality of the Absolute Truth are unlimited. Clearly, to be truly infinite God must be not only quantitatively but also qualitatively infinite. Unfortunately, in our mechanistic, industrial age we tend to define infinity only in its quantitative sense, and thus we fail to notice that an infinity of personal qualities is a necessary aspect of infinity. In other words, God must have infinite beauty, infinite wealth, infinite intelligence, infinite humor, infinite kindness, infinite anger and so on. Infinite is an absolute, and if anything we observe in this world is not contained, somehow or other, within our conception of the Absolute, then that conception is of something limited and not of the Absolute at all.

Only in Kali-yuga are there philosophers foolish enough to proudly define the most absolute of all terms — God — in materialistic, relative ways and then declare themselves enlightened thinkers. No matter how big our brain may be, we should have the common sense to place it at the feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

492.words_en_fc: whose; name; a person who is dying; distressed; collapsing; voice faltering; or; helplessly; chanting; a person; freed; of fruitive work; from the chains; the topmost; destination; achieves; they do not worship; Him, the Personality of Godhead; in the Age of Kali; people.
492.yw_zh: 人之将死，诚惶诚恐，卧床不起。虽然他的声音支吾哽咽，难以意识到自己在说些什么，但如果他念诵至尊主的圣名，就能从他的果报活动的反应中超脱出来，到达至高无上的目的地。然而，尽管如此，卡历年代的人们却不会崇拜至尊主。
492.yw_en: Terrified, about to die, a man collapses on his bed. Although his voice is faltering and he is hardly conscious of what he is saying, if he utters the holy name of the Supreme Lord he can be freed from the reaction of his fruitive work and achieve the supreme destination. But still people in the Age of Kali will not worship the Supreme Lord.
492.yz_zh: 你可以把马引到水边，但不能强迫他饮水。
492.yz_en: You can lead a horse to water, but you cannot make him drink.

492.ldw:

 bhagavān puruṣottamaḥ|

492.words_en_fc: of men; created by the influence of Kali; the faults; objects; space; and personal nature; based upon; all; steals away; situated within the heart; the almighty Lord; the Supreme Person.
492.yw_zh: 在卡历-尤嘎，物体、地方、甚至个体性格全受到污染。然而，全能的人格首神能使内心专注于主的人，免除其生命中的一切污染。
492.yw_en: In the Kali-yuga, objects, places and even individual personalities are all polluted. The almighty Personality of Godhead, however, can remove all such contamination from the life of one who fixes the Lord within his mind.

492.yw_zh: 人若聆听、荣耀、冥想、崇拜或只是崇敬居于心中的至尊主，主将去除累计在他心中千万生世的不洁。
492.yw_en: If a person hears about, glorifies, meditates upon, worships or simply offers great respect to the Supreme Lord, who is situated within the heart, the Lord will remove from his mind the contamination accumulated during many thousands of lifetimes.

492.yw_zh: 就像烈火炼黄金，祛除其他金属杂质留下的任何变色痕迹，寓居于心中的主维施努可净化瑜伽师们的心意。
492.yw_en: Just as fire applied to gold removes any discoloration caused by traces of other metals, Lord Viṣṇu within the heart purifies the minds of the yogīs.
492.yz_zh: 虽然一个人也许修炼神秘瑜伽体系，但他实际的灵性进步是由于心中的至尊主的仁慈使然；并非直接是他苦行和冥想的结果。人若以瑜伽的名义变得自命不凡，那么，他的灵性地位便是荒谬可笑的。
492.yz_en: Although one may practice the mystic yoga system, his actual spiritual advancement is due to the mercy of the Supreme Lord within the heart; it is not directly the result of his austerity and meditation. If one becomes foolishly proud in the name of yoga, his spiritual position becomes ridiculous.

492.ldw:
